_comment: Curated recall fixture (spec v3 §10, items 16/17). Each case is a short, synthetic opinion snippet with its ground-truth expected flag categories. The recall gate (test_recall_fixture) enforces 100% recall for DISCREDITED_FORENSIC_METHOD across the known-forensic cases; other categories are reported (precision/recall printed) but not hard-gated, since real-world recall is a measured metric, not a merge blocker. Synthetic text only — never real defendants.

cases.text: A forensic odontologist offered bite-mark comparison testimony identifying the defendant as the source of the wound.
An analyst testified to a microscopic hair comparison matching hairs from the scene to the defendant.
The State introduced comparative bullet-lead analysis to link the recovered rounds to the defendant's box of ammunition.
The fire investigator relied on a pour pattern and alligatoring to declare the blaze incendiary.
The prosecution's theory rested on the shaken baby syndrome triad as proof of abuse.
The examiner gave firearm identification testimony, calling it a toolmark identification to the exclusion of all other guns.
A footwear impression from the scene was said to match the defendant's shoe to a certainty.
The lab reported a complex DNA mixture interpretation placing the defendant among several contributors.
A detective offered bloodstain pattern analysis to reconstruct the position of the victim.
The prosecution used a dog scent identification from a scent lineup to place the defendant at the scene.
A jailhouse informant testified against the defendant in exchange for leniency on his own charges.
The conviction turned on the sole eyewitness, who later recanted her identification.
The court found the prosecutor withheld exculpatory evidence from the defense in violation of Brady.
Officers planted evidence and falsified the police report to secure the arrest.
A sixteen-year-old juvenile was interrogated for nine hours overnight without a parent present.
The prosecutor used peremptory strikes to remove Black jurors because of their race.
The appellate court reviewed the record and affirmed the judgment of sentence in all respects.
